chapter.title: Electricity
chapter.subject: Science
chapter.description: This chapter covers electric current, potential difference, resistance, Ohm's Law, electric power, and household electric circuits.

topics.title: Electric Current
topics.content: Electric current is the flow of electric charge through a conductor.

topics.title: Potential Difference
topics.content: Potential difference is the work done in moving a unit charge from one point to another.

topics.title: Ohm's Law
topics.content: The current through a conductor is directly proportional to the potential difference across it, provided the temperature remains constant.

topics.title: Resistance
topics.content: Resistance is the opposition offered by a conductor to the flow of current.

topics.title: Electric Power
topics.content: Electric power is the rate at which electrical energy is consumed.

key_points: Electric current is measured using an ammeter.
Potential difference is measured using a voltmeter.
Resistance depends on material, length, area, and temperature.
Resistors in series have higher equivalent resistance.
Resistors in parallel have lower equivalent resistance.

examples.question: A current of 2 A flows through a resistor connected to a 10 V battery. Find the resistance.
A bulb consumes 60 W of power at 220 V. Find the current.

mcqs.question: The SI unit of electric current is:
The SI unit of resistance is:

numericals.question: Calculate the current if 120 C of charge flows in 60 s.
Find the power consumed by a device operating at 12 V and drawing 3 A.
